z.$description:
Layering — a system-wide invariant (what covers what), not geometry. The
family declares the order; the values only have to preserve it. Before this
it was five magic numbers in five files: Menu and Tooltip both sat at 900,
so a tooltip on a menu item resolved by DOM order rather than by design;
Dialog and ConfirmDialog both sat at 1000, and ConfirmDialog opens from a
Dialog, so it worked only by accident of mount order; Toast, the one thing
that must float above everything, declared nothing at all. The order, least
to most interruptible: dropdown < tooltip < modal < modal-nested < palette <
onboarding < toast.

tooltip.$description: above dropdown, so a tooltip on a menu item wins

modal-nested.$description: ConfirmDialog, which opens from a Dialog

onboarding.$description: the coachmark; the scrim beneath it derives at the point of use as calc(var(--z-onboarding) - 10) — one slot, two uses

toast.$description: floats above everything, including onboarding — a transient notice raised by an action taken inside a dialog must stay visible
